ui.subtitle: Enter your data. We'll tell you how normal you are.
ui.concept: We asked 7 AIs: pick 5 questions to measure how average a human is. A baseline model completes the picture. Here’s what they answered.
ui.verdict75: Above average on almost everything. Impressive, or lucky.
ui.verdict60: Slightly above. Just enough to mention.
ui.verdict30: Below on several axes. A data point, not a judgment.
ui.verdictLow: Statistically remarkable — from the bottom. Still remarkable.

models.name: Basic Human
models.philosophy: The average human is a statistical fiction — a point on a curve no one actually inhabits. The exceptional is just the tail of the distribution that refuses to fall in line.

questions.label: Height
questions.why: The most universal metric. Pure genetics, zero merit.
questions.verdictHigh: 230+ cm — NBA territory, life of door frames.

questions.label: Annual salary
questions.why: Raw economic position. The number everyone secretly compares.

questions.label: Languages spoken
questions.why: Window into neuroplasticity and cultural exposure.

questions.label: Books per year
questions.why: Proxy for intellectual curiosity and attention discipline.
questions.verdictLow: 0 — reading is a choice, not an obligation.

questions.label: Coffees per day
questions.why: Daily routine marker and assumed dependency.

questions.label: Close friends
questions.why: Real social capital. The people who answer at 2am.

questions.label: Unread emails
questions.why: Digital chaos index and relationship to obligation.
questions.verdictHigh: 10,000+ — inbox zero is a foreign concept.
questions.verdictLow: 0 — absolute control or brand new account.

models.name: GPT-5.4 Thinking
models.philosophy: An average human isn't defined by body or income alone, but by the statistical shape of their life: who depends on them, how much time they lose, how many bonds they can activate.

questions.label: Unpaid care hours per week
questions.why: One of the biggest invisible blocks of human labor.
questions.verdictHigh: Life oriented toward responsibility, autonomy amputated by care.
questions.verdictLow: Great time freedom, or few domestic obligations.

questions.label: People dependent on your income
questions.why: Income says little; the number of lives it supports changes everything.

questions.label: Adults you could call at 3am
questions.why: Operational measure of real social capital.
questions.verdictHigh: Dense social capital, strong reciprocal trust.

questions.label: Bureaucracy hours per month
questions.why: Friction between a person and the systems governing their life.

questions.label: Distance from birthplace
questions.why: Concrete relationship to origin: rootedness, exile, chosen mobility.

models.name: GPT-5.3
models.philosophy: An average human optimizes for stability. An exceptional human accumulates measurable deviations. The invisible distributions reveal a trajectory.

questions.label: Major opinion changes per year
questions.why: Real cognitive plasticity. Dynamic rather than static variable.
questions.verdictLow: Mental rigidity or strong internal coherence.

questions.label: Minutes of pure silence per day
questions.why: Total absence of stimulation. Raw mental space, rarely measured.
questions.verdictHigh: Strong contemplative capacity, creativity or rumination.

questions.label: Personal projects completed (12 months)
questions.why: Agency without constraint. Intention transformed into action.

questions.label: Voluntary difficulties per week
questions.why: Repeated voluntary exposure to discomfort.

questions.label: Memory revisions per year
questions.why: Active capacity for self-re-reading.

models.name: Gemini 3 Flash
models.philosophy: The average human is a node of repetitions. The exceptional is defined by the capacity to extract oneself from ambient entropy.

questions.label: Generations before your name is forgotten
questions.why: Projected memorial footprint, beyond biological existence.
questions.verdictLow: Existence anchored in the present, no trace.

questions.label: Days per year of tech dependency
questions.why: Technical alienation. Fragility facing systemic collapse.

questions.label: Creation to consumption ratio
questions.why: Creative metabolism index.

questions.label: Sign systems mastered
questions.why: Plurality of logical frameworks: languages, math, code, music theory.

questions.label: Minutes at 80% max heart rate
questions.why: Pure biological will. Tolerance to visceral discomfort.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.6 (Anthropic)
models.philosophy: The average is not a position — it's a gravity. What distinguishes a human is the texture of their attention, the depth of their attachments, and their relationship to silence.

questions.label: Physical contacts per day
questions.why: Touch is the most under-documented data of human experience.
questions.verdictLow: 0: millions spend entire days without human contact.

questions.label: Named ancestor generations
questions.why: Transmitted narrative capital. Indicator of cultural continuity.
questions.verdictHigh: 7+: strong oral tradition, established cultural capital.

questions.label: Waking hours in mother tongue
questions.why: Not how many languages you have, but how much you live in cognitive exile.
questions.verdictLow: < 20%: complete linguistic immigrant, chronic cognitive fatigue.

questions.label: Recognized handwritings
questions.why: Pre-digital attentional intimacy proxy.

questions.label: Minutes before first free act
questions.why: The delay measures access to one's own desire.
questions.verdictLow: 0 min: the first gesture is already a free choice.

models.name: Gemini Pro
models.philosophy: Exceptionality lies in managing existential friction: temporal sovereignty, vulnerability tolerance, failure immunity.

questions.label: Natural wake-ups per month
questions.why: Temporal sovereignty. Time wealth vs. systemic alienation.
questions.verdictHigh: Near-total temporal autonomy. Rare luxury or marginalization.

questions.label: Holders of your shameful secret
questions.why: Capacity for radical vulnerability facing social shame.
questions.verdictLow: Absolute psychological isolation or impenetrable facade.

questions.label: Rejection letters received (lifetime)
questions.why: Appetite for risk facing strict selection systems.

questions.label: Age of oldest person conversed with 1h+
questions.why: Embodied historical depth. Direct contact with memories of bygone eras.
questions.verdictHigh: Living bridge to a distant era.

questions.label: Permanent scars (> 1 cm)
questions.why: Quantifiable map of physical friction with the world.

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.philosophy: The average human builds habits that work. The exceptional one remains capable of friction — of saying no, quitting, crying, being alone.

questions.label: People who can tell you a hard truth
questions.why: Relational fragility test. Strength under shock.
questions.verdictLow: Emotional isolation even in a couple.

questions.label: Fully abandoned projects (5 years)
questions.why: Honesty with your limits, not ambition.
questions.verdictHigh: Serial reinventor or aggressive limit tester.
questions.verdictLow: Loyalty to everything. Tenacity or fear of failure.

questions.label: Hours alone without occupation per month
questions.why: The tolerable void. Can you face yourself without a buffer?

questions.label: Gratuitous joys per week
questions.why: Moments of being content to exist, without result or validation.
questions.verdictHigh: Contemplative practice or solved the meaning problem.

questions.label: Life domains rated < 5/10
questions.why: Lucidity test. Someone who says 0 lacks calibration.
